findings.signature: WITH RECURSIVE causal_walk(origin_id, node_id, hop_distance, walk_score) AS (...)
findings.notes: Recursive CTE for graph traversal (2-hop). Turso lacks WITH RECURSIVE — must be replaced with iterative application-side traversal or bounded UNION ALL.
better-sqlite3 Statement type cast used for .all()/.get() — Turso driver has different statement API (async, no .raw()/.pluck()/.iterate()).
Synchronous .all() with spread params. Turso/libSQL uses async execute() with different param binding.
Recursive CTE for transitive dependency closure (cycle detection + invalidation). Turso lacks WITH RECURSIVE — must rewrite as iterative BFS.

findings.signature: database.exec(`CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS ...; CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS ...`)
findings.notes: Multi-statement exec() for DDL. Turso supports exec() but semicolon-separated batching may behave differently.
INSERT OR IGNORE syntax. Supported in Turso but verify conflict resolution semantics match.
SQLite datetime() function in DDL default. Turso supports but verify expression default compatibility.
PRAGMA table_info for dynamic column introspection. Turso/libSQL lacks .pragma() API and PRAGMA results may differ — used at 3 sites in this file.
better-sqlite3 synchronous transaction API. Turso uses async batch() or imperative BEGIN/COMMIT.
Second PRAGMA table_info site for bulk insert column discovery. Same Turso gap as line 382.
datetime('now') in UPDATE SET. Supported in Turso but verify expression evaluation.
Third PRAGMA table_info site in readEdges (sweep module call path). Dynamic column discovery pattern repeated across causal layer.

findings.signature: SELECT 1 AS present FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name = ?
findings.notes: Direct sqlite_master query for table existence. Turso may require information_schema or different introspection path.

findings.signature: SELECT 1 AS present FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='index' AND name = ?
findings.notes: Direct sqlite_master query for index existence verification. Same Turso introspection gap.
AUTOINCREMENT keyword in tombstone DDL. Turso supports but AUTOINCREMENT semantics (monotonicity guarantee) may differ in distributed context.
better-sqlite3 lastInsertRowid (bigint). Turso returns inserted row ID differently via execute() result.
Fourth PRAGMA table_info site in frontmatter promoter. Same Turso introspection gap — pattern is pervasive across causal layer.

findings.signature: SELECT ... FROM memory_index WHERE spec_folder IN (...) AND COALESCE(parent_id, 0) = 0
findings.notes: Complex SELECT with COALESCE in WHERE. Supported in Turso but verify expression evaluation parity.
Custom transaction wrapper used alongside better-sqlite3 native .transaction(). Turso migration must unify transaction API.
Module-level db reference via better-sqlite3 Database type. Turso migration requires replacing Database.Database with libSQL client type across all causal modules.
